methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Stratek Global is a growth-stage nuclear energy company founded by Kelvin Kemm. The valuation universe comprises eight private peers and three public comparables in the nuclear energy sector, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy among private firms, and public tickers OKLO, NNE, and SMR.

Standard peer-median methodology is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage comparables—zero companies matched both stage and valuation tier criteria. The broader peer set includes two adjacent-stage companies and eight same-sector peers overall, but this falls short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable peer triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the wide variance in technology maturity, capital requirements, and commercialization timelines within nuclear energy.

Without a sufficiently dense comparable set at Stratek's specific stage and valuation tier, traditional multiple-based approaches cannot be applied with confidence. Alternative methods—including discounted cash flow analysis tied to development milestones, or cost-of-capital approaches anchored to sector-specific risk profiles—would require additional operational and financial data not provided. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of granular comparables at the exact stage and scale intersection. Any valuation recommendation would require either expanded peer identification within nuclear energy's growth segment or supplemental primary data on Stratek's technical progress, capital efficiency, and pathway to commercialization.
